The presence of sparse, sclerophyllous shrubs and scattered Eucalyptus/Acacia species (likely Mulga or similar arid-zone Acacias) combined with the red sandy soil and clumps of spinifex-like grasses is a diagnostic botanical and pedological signature of central Australia.

The combination of LHT and the specific infrastructure design strongly supports Australia.

The street lighting poles are highly characteristic of rural Australia, specifically the slim metal poles with solar panels and the specific curved arm design.
